description: The 2004 voluntary recall of Merck’s Vioxx (painkiller) after studies showed it significantly increased the risk of heart attacks and strokes. The event led to massive litigation and a shift in how long-term safety data is monitored.

key_events.description: Data from the APPROVe trial shows clear cardiovascular dangers.
Merck publicly announces a worldwide withdrawal of the drug.
The Lancet publishes an editorial criticizing Merck for suppression of earlier warnings.
Initial class-action lawsuits are filed by thousands of former users.

calibration_notes: Tests the speed and severity of public opinion collapse following a major industry failure.
